questions.text: Fact 1: A boy has four cars.Fact 2: Two of the cars are red.Fact 3: He has one sports car. If the above three statements are facts then which of the following statement will also be a fact? I. The boy has a red sports car.II. He has 2 corollas.III. His favorite color is red.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Only statements I and II are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because neither statement I nor statement II can be confirmed as a fact based on the given information.

This option is incorrect because statement II, "He has 2 Corollas," cannot be confirmed as a fact based on the given information. As explained above, there is no information about the specific makes or models of the boy's cars, including whether he has any Corollas. Hence, this statement cannot be considered a fact.

This option is incorrect because statement III, "His favorite color is red," cannot be confirmed as a fact based on the given information. The facts provided are specific to the number of cars he has, the colors of two of the cars, and the presence of one sports car.

options.text: None of the statements is a fact
options.explanation: There is not enough information to support any of the statements. The boy has a sports car but it is not known which of his vehicles is red. The brands of the cars are not given hence there is no way to prove that two of his cars are corollas. There is no way to know if the boy's favorite color is red just because he has two red cars.

questions.explanation: There is not enough information to support any of the statements. The boy has a sports car but it is not known which of his vehicles is red. The brands of the cars are not given hence there is no way to prove that two of his cars are corollas. There is no way to know if the boy's favorite color is red just because he has two red cars.

questions.text: Statements:I. The farmers have decided against selling their Kharif crops to the government agencies.II. The government has reduced the procurement price of Kharif crops starting from last month to the next six months.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the order of cause and effect is reversed. Statement II (reducing the procurement price) is the cause that led to Statement I (farmers deciding against selling their crops to the government agencies), not the other way around.

options.text: Statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: The reduction in the procurement price of crops must have instigated the farmers not to sell their produce to government agencies.

options.text: Both statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the two statements have a clear cause-and-effect relationshipThis option is incorrect because there is a cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements, but the relationship is not one where both statements are effects of independent causes.. Statement II (reducing the procurement price) directly influences the decision made by the farmers as described in Statement I.

options.text: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because there is a cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements, but the relationship is not one where both statements are effects of independent causes.

questions.explanation: The reduction in the procurement price of crops must have instigated the farmers not to sell their produce to government agencies.

questions.text: Statement: Without reforming the entire administrative system, we cannot eradicate corruption and prejudice from society.Assumptions:I. The existence of corruption and prejudice is good.II. There is enough flexibility to change the administrative system.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because assumption I, "The existence of corruption and prejudice is good," is not implicit in the statement. The statement does not express any positive view of corruption and prejudice.

The statement talks of eradicating corruption and prejudice from society, which indicates that these aspects are undesirable. The existence of corruption and prejudice is not good as they create unfair opportunities for different groups. So, the assumption I is not implicit. Besides, the statement mentions reforming the administrative system. So, assumption Il is implicit.

options.text: Either assumption I or II is implicit
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because assumption II, "There is enough flexibility to change the administrative system," is implicit in the statement, as explained above. Assumption I, "The existence of corruption and prejudice is good," is not implicit in the statement, and therefore, this option is not valid.

options.text: Both Options A and B are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because only assumption II, "There is enough flexibility to change the administrative system," is implicit in the statement. The assumption I, "The existence of corruption and prejudice is good," is not implicit in the statement, as discussed earlier.

questions.explanation: The statement talks of eradicating corruption and prejudice from society, which indicates that these aspects are undesirable. The existence of corruption and prejudice is not good as they create unfair opportunities for different groups. So, the assumption I is not implicit. Besides, the statement mentions reforming the administrative system. So, assumption II is implicit.

questions.text: Statements and actions:Statement A smoker has left smoking suddenly 1. He will feel restlessness and anxiety. 2. He will start gaining weight
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: a) Both of them follow (Option a):Option (a) suggests that both statements 1 and 2 follow as consequences of the smoker suddenly quitting smoking.

b) None of them follow (Option b):Option (b) suggests that neither statement 1 nor statement 2 follows as consequences of the smoker suddenly quitting smoking.This option implies that the smoker will not experience restlessness, anxiety, or weight gain after quitting smoking.
c) Only b follows (Option c):Option (c) suggests that only statement 2 follows as a consequence of the smoker suddenly quitting smoking.
a smoker when he left smoking suddenly there will be no effect on his weight but he will start feeling anxious and restless. So only option A follows.

questions.explanation: a smoker when he left smoking suddenly there will be no effect on his weight but he will start feeling anxious and restless. So only option A follows.

questions.text: Statement:The Management of School M has decided to give free breakfast from next academic year to all the students in its primary section through its canteen even though they will not get any government grant.Courses of Action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's analyze each course of action in response to the given statement:Statement: The Management of School M has decided to give free breakfast from next academic year to all the students in its primary section through its canteen even though they will not get any government grant.Courses of Action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked, and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.Let's evaluate each course of action:I. The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.This course of action suggests that offering free breakfast to all students in the primary section may attract many poor students to seek admission at the school. Providing free breakfast could be a positive incentive for economically disadvantaged families to consider enrolling their children in the school.II. The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked, and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.This course of action suggests that the canteen facilities and utensils need to be assessed and upgraded to ensure they can cater to the increased demand for providing free breakfast to all primary students. Proper equipment and facilities are essential for efficiently running the breakfast program.III. Funds will have to be raised to support the scheme for years to come.This course of action highlights the need for raising funds to sustain the free breakfast program in the long term. Offering free breakfast to all primary students will involve ongoing costs for procuring food items and maintaining the canteen, especially if the school is not receiving any government grant for the initiative.To summarize, the courses of action outline some steps that the school management needs to consider after deciding to provide free breakfast to all primary students. These steps include potentially admitting more students, checking and upgrading the canteen facilities, and raising funds to sustain the program. The success of the initiative will depend on effective planning and resource management by the school management.
Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.
Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.
Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.

questions.explanation: Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.

questions.text: Statement:The availability of imported fruits has increased in the indigenous market and so the demand for indigenous fruits has been decreased. Courses of Action:I. To help the indigenous producers of fruits, the Government should impose a high import duty on these fruits, even if they are not of good quality.II. The fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits. So that the demand for indigenous fruits would increase.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is wrong because Course I suggests supporting low-quality fruits, which is unreasonable and harmful to consumers.
This option is wrong because stopping vendors from selling imported fruits is impractical and against free market choice.

The ideas suggested in both courses of action I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better-quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follow.
This option is wrong because neither course is practical or logical.

questions.explanation: The ideas suggested in both I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better-quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follow.

questions.text: Statements: Some papers are pens. Angle is a paper.Conclusions:I. Angle is not a pen.II. Angle is a pen.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because conclusion II, "Angle is a pen," does follow from the given statements.

options.text: Either conclusion I or II follows
options.explanation: From the information given we know that angle is a paper. However, the first statement says that "some" papers are pens which implies that the papers (which are the same as the angle) may or may not be a pen hence either one of the statements could be right.

options.text: Neither conclusions I nor II follow
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because conclusion II, "Angle is a pen," does follow from the given statements. As explained earlier, the second statement "Angle is a paper" provides direct information about Angle's identity, and the relationship established in the first statement allows us to conclude that Angle is indeed a pen.

options.text: Both conclusion I and II follow
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because conclusion I, "Angle is not a pen," does not follow from the given statements. As mentioned before, the first statement "Some papers are pens" doesn't provide enough information to determine whether Angle is not a pen.

questions.explanation: From the information given we know that angle is a paper. However, the first statement says that “some” papers are pens which implies that the papers (which are the same as the angle) may or may not be a pen hence either one of the statements could be right.

questions.text: Statement: A large number of people have become critically ill after consuming spurious liquor from a local shop. Courses of Action: I. The Government should immediately close down all the shops selling liquor till the stocks are tested for the presence of toxicity. II. The owner of the liquor shop should be asked to leave the town and open a shop elsewhere. Ill. The owner of the liquor shop should immediately be arrested and tried for criminal negligence.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Clearly, the owner of the shop should be punished for selling spurious liquor. Further, attempts should be made to unearth other such unscrupulous liquor sellers by conducting raids and testing samples collected from different shops. So, both I and III follow. However, sending the liquor shop owner to another place may create a problem elsewhere also. So, II does not follow.

Further, attempts should be made to unearth other such unscrupulous liquor sellers by conducting raids and testing samples collected from different shops. So, both I and III follow. However, sending the liquor shop owner to another place may create a problem elsewhere also. So, II does not follow.

However, sending the liquor shop owner to another place may create a problem elsewhere also. So, II does not follow.

questions.text: Statements and Actions:Statement The availability of imported fruits has increased in the indigenous market and so the demand for indigenous fruits has been decreased. I. To help the indigenous producers of fruits, the Government should impose high import duty on these fruits, even if these are not of good quality. II. The fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits. So that the demand for indigenous fruits would be increased. Choose the correct option.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option is incorrect.Option a) Both of them followExplanation: This option implies that both I and II are valid actions to address the decreased demand for indigenous fruits due to the increased availability of imported fruits. In other words, both actions would help in promoting the consumption of indigenous fruits.

Neither of the options follows.Option 1 suggests imposing high import duty on fruits, even if they are of bad quality, which may not be a suitable solution.Option 2 suggests stopping the sale of imported fruits, which may not be feasible and may not address the root cause of the problem.

This option is incorrect.Option c) Only I followsExplanation: This option indicates that only I is a valid action to take based on the given statement. It suggests that imposing high import duties on imported fruits, even if they are of low quality, would help support indigenous fruit producers and potentially increase the demand for their fruits.

This option is incorrect.Option d) Only II followsExplanation: This option suggests that only II is a valid action based on the given statement. It proposes that fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits, leading to an increase in demand for indigenous fruits.

questions.explanation: The ideas suggested in both I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.

Courses of action: I) The government of Pakistan should immediately put a complete ban On people coming to Pakistan from country X including those Pakistanis who are settled in country X. II) The government of Pakistan should immediately set up detection centres at all its airports and seaports to identify and quarantine those who are tested positive.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: If only I is most appropriate
If only II is most appropriate
If either I or II is most appropriate
If neither I nor II is most appropriate
if both I and II are most appropriate

questions.text: Statements and Actions:Statement: According to reports children below age 10 are being aggressive because of mobile phonesI. Parents need to be strict with their children.II. Parents need to be concerned and limit their screening time. Which follows?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Option a) Both of them followExplanation: This option implies that both I and II are valid conclusions drawn from the statement. In other words, the statement supports the ideas presented in both options.

Option b) None of them followExplanation: This option indicates that neither I nor II can be concluded from the statement. In other words, the statement does not provide any evidence to support either of the options.

Option c) Only I followsExplanation: This option suggests that only I can be concluded from the statement. It means that the statement supports the idea that parents need to be strict with their children, but it does not necessarily support the idea in option II.

Only statement 2 follows. Statement 2 suggests that parents need to be concerned and limit their children's screen time, which is a more plausible solution to address the issue of children being aggressive due to mobile phone usage.

questions.explanation: The root cause of children getting aggressive is their mobile phones hence limiting the time that children use their phones can solve this problem. Lenient parentage is not the problem plus strict parenting sometimes causes rebellious behavior.

questions.text: Statement: The availability of imported fruits has increased in the indigenous market and so the demand for indigenous fruits has been decreased. I. To help the indigenous producers of fruits, the Government should impose high import duty on these fruits, even if these are not of good quality. II. The fruit vendors should stop selling imported fruits. So that the demand for indigenous fruits would be increased.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Both Statements I and II represent unfair means to cut competition.It is an incorrect option.

The ideas suggested in both Statements I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.

Imposing high import duty on imported fruits might make them more expensive, but it can also lead to negative consequences, such as an increase in the price of fruits for consumers and potential retaliation from trading partners. Additionally, imposing high import duties on low-quality fruits might not necessarily boost the demand for indigenous fruits if consumers still prefer the taste and quality of imported ones.

If fruit vendors stop selling imported fruits, it might indeed increase the demand for indigenous fruits. However, it could also limit consumer choices and preferences. Some consumers might prefer imported fruits for various reasons, such as taste, variety, or nutritional content.

questions.explanation: The ideas suggested in both Statements I and II represent unfair means to cut competition. The correct way would be to devise methods and techniques such that the indigenous producers could produce better quality fruits and make them available in the market at prices comparable with those of the imported ones. Hence, neither I nor II follows.

questions.text: Dependent Causes/ Independent Causes: Statement: All the schools in the area had to be kept closed for most of the week. Many parents have withdrawn their children from the local schools.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because there is no clear cause-and-effect relationship between the two statements. The closure of schools (Statement I) doesn't directly lead to parents withdrawing their children (Statement II).

options.text: Statement II is the cause, and statement I is its effect
options.explanation: This option is also incorrect because there is no evidence to suggest that parents withdrawing their children (Statement II) is causing the closure of schools (Statement I). If anything, it would be the other way around—if the schools were closed, some parents might choose to withdraw their children, but again, it's not a direct cause-and-effect relationship.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements are describing independent causes that are not directly linked to each other. The closure of schools (Statement I) could be due to reasons such as extreme weather conditions, a health-related emergency, or a labor strike by teachers, among other possibilities.

options.text: Both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes
options.explanation: Both statements I and II are effects of independent causes. This option is correct, and it summarizes the relationship between the two statements accurately. Both events seem to be the effects of different independent causes, rather than one causing the other.

questions.explanation: "Closing the schools for a week and the parents withdrawing their wards from the local schools are independent issues", which must have been triggered by different individual causes.

questions.text: Statement: There is an unprecedented increase in the migration of villagers to urban areas as repeated crop failure has put them into a precarious financial situation.Courses of Action:I. The villagers should be provided with an alternate source of income in their villages which will make them stay put.II. The migrated villagers should be provided with jobs in the urban areas to help them survive.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Correct because providing alternate sources of income in villages directly addresses the root cause (crop failure and financial distress) and helps prevent migration.
Wrong because providing jobs in cities accepts migration instead of solving the cause, and does not reduce rural distress.
Wrong because Course II is not an appropriate corrective action; only Course I is logical
Wrong because Course II does not solve the problem stated; hence both do not follow.

questions.explanation: Action I addresses the root cause of the problem (lack of income in villages) and is a sustainable, long-term solution. Action II is a temporary fix that could encourage more migration and further strain urban resources. Therefore, Action I is the more logical course.

questions.text: Statement:Should coal engines be replaced by electric engines in trains?Arguments:(I) Yes. Coal engines cause a lot of pollution.(II) Yes. Electric engines are good on performance, easy to operate, and low on maintenance.(III) No. Pakistan does not produce enough electricity to fulfill its domestic needs also.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: this is not a suitable option. Argument III: "No. Pakistan does not produce enough electricity to fulfill its domestic needs also." While this might be a valid concern, it doesn't directly address the question of whether coal engines should be replaced. It introduces a practical limitation, but not a fundamental reason why electric engines are unsuitable. Therefore, it is not as strong an argument as I and II.

Arguments I and II seem to be true but argument III does not provide a convincing reason and hence does not hold strong.

questions.text: Statements:All film stars are playback singers.All film directors are film stars.Conclusions:I. All film directors are playback singers.II. Some film stars are film directors.Which of the following is true?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because conclusion I does not necessarily follow. The statements only state that all film directors are film stars, but it doesn't guarantee that they are also playback singers.
Option B is incorrect because both conclusions are not valid. Conclusion I is not necessarily true, and conclusion II is already given in the statements.
Option C is correct because neither conclusion I nor II follows with certainty from the given statements.

Since both the premises are universal and affirmative, the conclusion must be universal affirmative and should not contain the middle term. So, 1 follow. Il is the converse of the second premise and so it also holds.

questions.explanation: Since both the premises are universal and affirmative, the conclusion must be universal affirmative and should not contain the middle term. So, I follow. II is the converse of the second premise and so it also holds.

questions.text: Statement:The Management of School M has decided to give free breakfast from next academic year to all the students in its primary section through its canteen even though they will not get any government grant.Courses of Action(I) The school will have to admit many poor students who will seek admission for the next academic year.(II) The canteen facilities and utensils have to be checked and new purchases to be made to equip it properly.(III) Funds will have to be raised to support the sc heme for years to come.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.

Option B is incorrect because only conclusion I follows. The statement mentions that the school will provide free breakfast to all students in the primary section; there is no indication about admitting many poor students.

Option C is incorrect because only conclusion I and II follow. The statement supports the provision of free breakfast and the need for checking canteen facilities and utensils, but it doesn't specifically mention raising funds.

Option D is incorrect because both conclusion I and II follow. The statement clearly states that the school management decided to provide free breakfast to all students in the primary section, which may lead to admitting more students, and it implies the need for checking canteen facilities. However, there is no direct mention of raising funds.

questions.explanation: Since the school has introduced the scheme without receiving any grant, it needs to pool up the funds and make adequate arrangements to keep the scheme going. So, only II and III follow.

questions.text: Statement:I. Large number of Primary Schools in rural areas are run by only one teacher.II. There has been a huge dropout from primary schools in rural areas.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Statement I is the cause then 2 is its effect
options.explanation: One teacher running a lot of schools leads to mismanagement and it lowers the educational standard which causes students to drop out from school in rural areas. So, option A is the most accurate answer.

options.text: Statement 2 is the cause then I is its effect
options.explanation: Option B (Statement 2 is the cause, then I is its effect) is incorrect because the dropout from primary schools in rural areas can have various causes, and there is no indication that it directly causes the large number of schools run by one teacher.

Option C (Both statements are independent causes) is incorrect because the two statements do not appear to have any direct connection or correlation. They describe different situations without any clear causal relationship.

options.text: Both of the statements are effect of independent causes
options.explanation: Option D (Both of the statements are effects of independent causes) is incorrect because there is no evidence provided to suggest that both statements are effects of distinct, unrelated causes. Again, they seem to describe different aspects of primary education in rural areas without any evident causal link.

questions.explanation: One teacher running a lot of schools leads to mismanagement and it lowers the educational standard which causes students to drop out from school in rural areas. So, option A is the most accurate answer.

questions.text: Statement: The ratio of poverty is at an alarming point in our country. Conclusions:I. The Government needs to take steps for economic and development growth. II. The lower class area of people in our country needs to be supported as most of them live hand to mouth.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: As there are alot of poverty in the country,the government needs to take steps for economic growth so that people get job opportunities and are able to get out of poverty.Economic growth is the most powerful instrument for reducing poverty and improving the quality of life in developing countries.Secondly,the lower class area of people in our country need to be supported as most of them live hand to mouth.Hence,both of the conclusions follow.

Explanation: The statement only mentions that the poverty ratio is alarming, but it does not provide any specific information about the reasons or solutions for it. Therefore, we cannot draw any conclusions from the given statement.

Explanation: Since the statement talks about the alarming level of poverty in the country, it suggests that the Government needs to take steps for economic and development growth to address the issue of poverty effectively.

Explanation: The statement mentions that the poverty ratio is alarming, implying that the lower-class people might be more affected. Therefore, it suggests the need to support these vulnerable groups who live hand to mouth.

questions.explanation: As there is a lot of poverty in the country, the government needs to take steps for economic growth so that people get job opportunities and are able to get out of poverty. Economic growth is the most powerful instrument for reducing poverty and improving the quality of life in developing countries.Secondly, the lower class area of people in our country needs to be supported as most of them live hand to mouth.Hence, both of the conclusions follow.

questions.text: Directions:In each of the following questions a statement is given, followed by two conclusions.Statement: "Please do not wait for me, I may be late, start taking lunch as soon as the guests arrive." - A message from a Director of a Company to his office managers.Assumptions:I. Keeping guests waiting is not desirable.II. Lunch may not be ready in time.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The director instructs his managers not to keep the guests waiting because of him and to proceed with lunch soon after their arrival. This implies that lunch would be ready in time. So, only I is implicit. Hence, option A is accurate.

Option B is incorrect because assumption II is not implicit in the given statement. There is no indication in the statement that lunch may not be ready in time. The director only mentions being late, not the unavailability of lunch.

options.text: Either I or II is implicit
options.explanation: Option C is incorrect because although assumption I is implicit, assumption II is not implicit, as explained above.
Option D is incorrect because it includes option B, which is itself incorrect. Therefore, option D cannot be the correct answer.

questions.explanation: The director instructs his managers not to keep the guests waiting because of him and to proceed with lunch soon after their arrival. This implies that lunch would be ready in time. So, only I is implicit. Hence, option A is accurate.

questions.text: The Government has decided not to provide financial support to voluntary organizations from the next Five Year Plan and has communicated that all such organizations should raise funds to meet their financial needs. questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

Hence, it is not a good course of action.If we follow Course of action 2, it implies that the fruit vendors stop selling imported fruits to promote their native fruit market. It may be a little helpful in the short run, but it is not a practical solution. Hence, it is also not a good course of action.Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.text: Statements and Actions:Statement I There has been an unprecedented increase in the number of requests for seats in most of the long-distance trains during the current holiday season.Statement II There is an unprecedented increase in the migration of villagers to urban areas as repeated crop failure has put them into a precarious financial situation. Courses of ActionI. The villagers should be provided with alternative sources of income in their villages which will make them stay put.II. The migrated villagers should be provided with jobs in the urban areas to help them survive. authority should immediately increase the capacity in each of these trains by attaching additional coaches.II. The people seeking accommodation should be advised to make their travel plan after the holiday.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The given statements and courses of action are as follows:Statement: There has been an unprecedented increase in the number of requests for seats in most of the long-distance trains during the current holiday season.I. The Statement: There is an unprecedented increase in the migration of villagers to urban areas as repeated crop failure has put them into a precarious financial situation.Courses of Action:I. The villagers should be provided with alternative sources of income in their villages which will make them stay put.II. The migrated villagers should be provided with jobs in the urban areas to help them survive.III. The authority should immediately increase the capacity in each of these trains by attaching additional coaches.IV. The people seeking accommodation should be advised to make their travel plan after the holiday.Let's evaluate each course of action:I. The course of action suggests providing alternative sources of income to the villagers in their villages to encourage them to stay. This action aims to address the root cause of migration, which is the precarious financial situation resulting from repeated crop failures. This action could potentially help reduce migration by improving the economic conditions in the villages.II. The course of action suggests providing jobs to the migrated villagers in urban areas to help them survive. This action recognizes the existing migration and aims to support those who have already migrated. By providing employment opportunities, it can help improve the livelihoods of the migrated villagers.III. The course of action proposes increasing the capacity of trains by attaching additional coaches to accommodate the increased number of requests for seats. This action directly addresses the issue mentioned in the statement and aims to meet the increased demand for train travel during the holiday season.IV. The course of action suggests advising people seeking accommodation to plan their travel after the holiday season. This action aims to manage the demand for accommodation during the holiday season by encouraging people to adjust their travel plans.In conclusion, among the given courses of action, the actions presented in options I, II, and III seem relevant and appropriate. These actions address the underlying causes of migration, provide support to the migrated villagers, and directly respond to the increased demand for train travel. Option IV, while a valid suggestion, may not directly address the issues mentioned in the statement.
Since all of them follows this option is incorrect.

questions.text: Statement: The car dealer found that there was a tremendous response for the new XYZ car, with long queues of people complaining about the duration of business hours and arrangements. Courses of Action: I. People should make their own arrangements for lunch and snacks while going for the booking and be ready to spend several hours. II. The dealer should make arrangements for more booking desks and increased business hours to serve more people in less time.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Seeing the tremendous response, the dealer must make suitable arrangements and deploy more personnel to take care of customers so that they don't have to wait excessively long for booking. So, only course II follows.

Seeing the tremendous response, the dealer must make suitable arrangements and deploy more personnel to take care of customers so that they don't have to wait excessively long for booking. So, only course II follows.

Seeing the tremendous response, the dealer must make suitable arrangements and deploy more personnel to take care of customers so that they don't have to wait excessively long for booking. So, only course II follows.

Seeing the tremendous response, the dealer must make suitable arrangements and deploy more personnel to take care of customers so that they don't have to wait excessively long for booking. So, only course II follows.

questions.explanation: Action I unfairly places the burden on the customers. The logical business response to high demand and customer complaints is to improve service by increasing capacity, such as adding more desks and extending hours (Action II).

questions.text: In this question, a statement is given followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. It is required to grasp the statement, analyze the problem or policy it mentions and then decide which of the courses of action logically follow / most appropriate.Statement:The committee has criticized the institute for its failure to implement a dozen of regular programmes despite an increase in the staff strength and not drawing up a firm action plan for studies and research.Courses of action:I) The broad objectives of the Institute should be redefined to implement a practical action plan.II) The Institute should give a report on reasons for not having implemented the planned programmes.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Redefining the broad objectives and implementing a practical action plan directly addresses the committee's criticism of the institute's lack of a firm action plan.
Providing a report explains the past failure but does not directly address the need for future implementation.

options.text: If either I or II follows
options.explanation: Both actions are needed to fully address the issues raised by the committee; thus, choosing either one alone is insufficient.

options.text: If neither I nor II follows
options.explanation: Both actions are needed to respond appropriately to the committee's criticisms, so this option is incorrect.

options.text: If both I and II follow
options.explanation: Both courses of action are necessary: redefining objectives and implementing a practical plan addresses the committee's concerns, while providing a report explains the past failures, helping to avoid future issues.

questions.explanation: Both courses of action are necessary: redefining objectives and implementing a practical plan addresses the committee's concerns, while providing a report explains the past failures, helping to avoid future issues.

questions.text: In this question, a statement is given followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. It is required to grasp the statement, analyze the problem or policy it mentions and then decide which of the courses of action logically follow / most appropriate.Statement:Mr. X, an active member of the Union, often insults his superiors in the office with his rude behavior.Courses of action:I) He should be transferred to some other department.II) The matter should be referred to the Union.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Transferring Mr. X to another department may not address the root of his behavior and could simply shift the problem elsewhere.
Referring the matter to the Union might escalate the issue rather than resolve the behavioral problem directly with Mr. X.

options.text: If either I or II follows
options.explanation: Neither course of action independently addresses the underlying issue effectively, so considering them individually is not sufficient.

options.text: If neither I nor II follows
options.explanation: Both suggested courses of action fail to tackle the actual problem of Mr. X's behavior, and therefore, neither is appropriate.

options.text: If both I and II follow
options.explanation: Implementing both actions still does not focus on correcting Mr. X's behavior directly, making this option inadequate.

questions.explanation: Both suggested courses of action fail to tackle the actual problem of Mr. X's behavior, and therefore, neither is appropriate.

questions.text: In this question, a statement is given followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. It is required to grasp the statement, analyze the problem or policy it mentions and then decide which of the courses of action logically follow / most appropriate.Statement:Majority of the students in many schools do not pass in the final examination.Courses of action:I) These schools should be closed down as these have become unproductive.II) The teachers of these schools should immediately be retrenched.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Closing down schools is a drastic measure that does not address the root cause of the problem. It is not a logical solution.
Retrenching teachers may not solve the problem of student performance and could be unfair without understanding the underlying issues.

options.text: If either I or II follows
options.explanation: Both courses of action are extreme and do not logically address the problem, so neither is an appropriate solution on its own.

options.text: If neither I nor II follows
options.explanation: Neither closing schools nor retrenching teachers directly addresses the issue of low student performance. Alternative measures should be considered.

options.text: If both I and II follow
options.explanation: Both actions are extreme and do not logically solve the underlying problem of student performance in exams.

questions.explanation: In evaluating the courses of action, it is essential to identify solutions that address the root causes of the problem effectively. The statement highlights a failure in student performance, which may be due to various factors such as teaching methods, curriculum, resources, or student engagement. Closing schools (Course of Action I) does not address these factors and is a disproportionate response. Retrenching teachers (Course of Action II) also does not necessarily improve student outcomes, as it fails to consider the reasons for poor performance. Therefore, neither of these actions is a logical solution, making Option D the correct choice. More constructive approaches would involve investigating the reasons behind the poor performance and implementing targeted interventions to improve educational outcomes.

questions.text: In this question, a statement is given followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. It is required to grasp the statement, analyze the problem or policy it mentions and then decide which of the courses of action logically follow / most appropriate.Statement:The Kharif crops have been affected by the insects for consecutive three years in the district and the farmers harvested less than fifty percent of produce during these years.Courses of action:I) The farmers should seek measures to control the attack of insects to protect their crops next year.II) The Government should increase the support price of Khalif crops considerably to protect the economic interests of farmers.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This implies that only the first course of action is logical. While controlling insects is crucial, it doesn't address the immediate economic needs of the farmers.
This implies that only the second course of action is logical. Increasing the support price helps economically but doesn't solve the root problem of insect attacks.

options.text: If either I or II follows
options.explanation: This implies that either of the actions alone is sufficient. However, both actions address different aspects of the problem.

options.text: If neither I nor II follows
options.explanation: This implies that neither action is suitable. This is incorrect as both actions address critical issues.

options.text: If both I and II follow
options.explanation: Both actions complement each other: I) addresses the root cause of the problem while II) provides immediate economic relief.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is E: Both I and II follow. The first course of action, I), focuses on long-term agricultural health by suggesting that farmers adopt strategies to combat insect infestations, which are responsible for the reduced harvest. The second course of action, II), is crucial for short-term economic stability, as increasing the support price can help farmers cope financially with the losses suffered over the past three years. Together, these actions form a comprehensive response to both the immediate and ongoing challenges faced by the farmers, making both courses of action logical and necessary.

questions.text: In this question, a statement is given followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. It is required to grasp the statement, analyze the problem or policy it mentions and then decide which of the courses of action logically follow / most appropriate.Statement:The local government has agreed to provide necessary infrastructural facilities to the proposed ABC trust's project of supply of clean water to city 'P'.Courses of action:I) The local government should provide necessary land to the trust by completing due formalitiesII) The local government should facilitate obtaining electricity and other permissions to the trust.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: While providing land is a part of infrastructural support, it alone does not fully encompass all necessary actions to support this project.
Facilitating electricity and permissions is crucial, but like land provision, it alone does not cover all necessary support.

options.text: If either I or II follows
options.explanation: Both actions are necessary to comprehensively support the project, so choosing only one is insufficient.

options.text: If neither I nor II follows
options.explanation: Ignoring both actions contradicts the commitment to provide infrastructural facilities.

options.text: If both I and II follow
options.explanation: Providing land and facilitating utilities and permissions together fulfill the local government's commitment to supporting the project.

questions.explanation: The statement indicates that the local government has agreed to provide necessary infrastructural facilities for the trust's clean water supply project. This commitment involves providing land (Course of Action I) and facilitating utilities and permissions (Course of Action II). Both actions are part of the infrastructural facilities needed to support the project effectively. Therefore, both I and II logically follow from the statement. Options A, B, C, and D are incorrect because they suggest that only one or neither of the actions is necessary, which would not fully meet the government's agreed support.

questions.text: In this question, a statement is given followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. It is required to grasp the statement, analyze the problem or policy it mentions and then decide which of the courses of action logically follow / most appropriate.Statement:The vegetable traders feel that the prices of onions will again go up shortly in the state A.Courses of action:I) The 'A' state government should purchase and store a sufficient quantity of onion in advance to control price.II) The 'A' state government should make an available network of fair-price shops for the sale of onions during periods of shortage.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Purchasing and storing onions can help stabilize prices by increasing supply during shortages.
Establishing fair-price shops can ensure onions are available at controlled prices during shortages.

options.text: If either I or II follows
options.explanation: Both actions independently address the issue of price control, but only one alone might not be sufficient.

options.text: If neither I nor II follows
options.explanation: Ignoring both actions may result in uncontrolled price hikes and scarcity.

options.text: If both I and II follow
options.explanation: Combining storage with fair-price outlets ensures both availability and price stability.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option E: If both I and II followCourse of Action I suggests that the state government should proactively purchase and store onions. This can help manage supply levels and prevent a significant price increase when shortages occur. Course of Action II suggests setting up fair-price shops, which would help ensure that onions are sold at an affordable price during shortages. Both actions together would address both the supply and pricing issues effectively. Implementing only one of these actions might not fully stabilize the market, making both necessary for comprehensive control. Hence, both actions are needed, and therefore, Option E is correct.

questions.text: In this question, a statement is given followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. It is required to grasp the statement, analyze the problem or policy it mentions and then decide which of the courses of action logically follow / most appropriate.Statement:The condition of NH-5 is too bad because of heavy transportation.Courses of action:I) The government should repair it immediately.II) The government should tell the pte to use an alternate road.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Repairing the road addresses the immediate problem of poor condition caused by heavy transportation.
Telling transporters to use an alternate route doesn't solve the problem of the road's current bad condition.

options.text: If either I or II follows
options.explanation: Both actions are not mutually exclusive solutions; only repairing directly addresses the issue.

options.text: If neither I nor II follows
options.explanation: At least one action is needed to address the bad condition of the road.

options.text: If both I and II follow
options.explanation: While both actions could be pursued, only repairing the road directly addresses the issue at hand.

questions.explanation: The correct course of action is to repair the road (Option A). The statement highlights the poor condition of NH-5 due to heavy transportation, which suggests that repairing it is a direct and necessary response. Option II suggests redirecting traffic, but this doesn't solve the problem of the existing bad condition of NH-5. Therefore, only the first course of action directly addresses the issue by repairing the road, making Option A the most appropriate choice. The other options fail to address the immediate problem effectively or suggest unnecessary measures.

questions.text: Statement: A large number of people die every year due to drinking polluted water during the summer.Courses of Action:I. The government should make adequate arrangements to provide safe drinking water to all its citizens.II. The people should be educated about the dangers of drinking polluted water.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Providing safe drinking water is essential, but alone it may not suffice if people are unaware of the risks. Hence, this option is not fully adequate.
Educating people is crucial, but without access to safe water, awareness alone cannot solve the problem. Therefore, this option does not fully address the issue.
Both actions are necessary in conjunction to effectively tackle the issue; hence, either one alone is insufficient.
Addressing the problem requires a dual approach: providing safe drinking water and raising awareness about the dangers of polluted water. Therefore, both actions are necessary.

questions.explanation: This problem requires a two-pronged approach. The government has a responsibility to provide a safe water supply (Action I), which is a long-term solution. Simultaneously, educating the public (Action II) empowers them to make safer choices and addresses the immediate risk.

questions.text: Statement: On average, about twenty people are run over by trains and die every day while crossing the railway tracks through the level crossing.Courses of Action:I. The railway authorities should be instructed to close all the level crossings.II. Those who are found crossing the tracks, when the gates are closed, should be fined heavily
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Closing all level crossings is impractical and would cause significant disruption to the public. Thus, this course of action is not viable.
Imposing heavy fines on those who cross tracks when gates are closed could deter such behavior and reduce accidents, making this a feasible solution.
Both actions do not equally address the problem, and only one is practical, so this option is incorrect.
One of the actions is practical and can help prevent accidents, so this option is incorrect.
Only one of the actions is practical and effective in reducing accidents, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The statement highlights a serious safety issue with people being run over by trains at level crossings. Closing all level crossings (Course of Action I) would be highly impractical and disrupt transportation significantly. However, imposing heavy fines on those who cross tracks when gates are closed (Course of Action II) is a feasible deterrent that could effectively reduce the number of accidents. Therefore, only Course of Action II follows. Option A is incorrect because closing all level crossings is not a viable solution. Option C is incorrect because not both actions can be considered equally effective. Option D is incorrect because one valid solution exists. Option E is incorrect because only one course of action is practical.

questions.text: Statement: Every year, at the beginning or at the end of the monsoons, we have some cases of conjunctivitis, but this year, it seems to be a major epidemic, witnessed after nearly four years.Courses of Action:I. Precautionary measures should be taken after every four years to check this epidemic.II. People should be advised to drink boiled water during rainy season.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Precautionary measures every four years do not address the annual occurrence of the disease.
Boiled water is not related to preventing conjunctivitis, which is an eye infection.
Neither action directly addresses the problem effectively, so this is not a viable choice.
Correct. Both actions are ineffective: conjunctivitis occurs annually, not just every four years, and boiled water does not prevent an eye infection.
Both courses of action fail to address the problem effectively or logically.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that neither I nor II follows. The issue of conjunctivitis arises annually, not just every four years, rendering the first course of action ineffective. Additionally, drinking boiled water is a preventive measure for waterborne diseases, not for an eye infection like conjunctivitis. Therefore, neither proposed course of action is logically sound in addressing the epidemic.

questions.text: Statement: There has been less than forty percent voter turnout in the recent assembly elections.Courses of Action:I. The election commission should cancel the entire election process as the votes cast are not adequate to represent people.II. The election commission should take away the voting rights of those who did not exercise their rights.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Canceling the election due to low turnout is impractical and costly, making this action not advisable.
Revoking voting rights would undermine democratic principles and is not a feasible solution.
Neither option is a reasonable or effective course of action, so this is incorrect.
Both proposed actions are impractical and do not address the root cause of low voter turnout, which requires positive engagement and awareness strategies.
Implementing both actions would be counterproductive and against democratic values, making this incorrect.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: Neither I nor II follows. Canceling the election due to low turnout would incur unnecessary expenses and does not guarantee higher participation in the future. Additionally, taking away voting rights from non-voters would permanently reduce the voter base and contradict democratic principles. A more effective course of action would be to enhance voter education and awareness to encourage higher participation in future elections. Therefore, neither of the proposed courses of action is appropriate.

questions.text: Statement: The officer in charge of a company had a hunch that some money was missing from the safe. Courses of action:I. He should get it recounted with the help of the staff and check it with the balance sheet.II. He should inform the police.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: If only I is most appropriate
options.explanation: Course of Action I:Recounting the money and cross-checking with the balance sheet is a logical first step. The officer's suspicion needs verification before any further action.

options.text: If only II is most appropriate
options.explanation: Course of Action II:Informing the police at this stage is premature. Without verifying the suspicion, it could lead to unnecessary complications.

options.text: If either I or II is most appropriate
options.explanation: Neither action is interchangeable. Verification (I) should precede escalation (II).

options.text: If neither I nor II is most appropriate
options.explanation: One of the actions is indeed appropriate. Verification (I) is necessary to confirm the suspicion.

questions.explanation: The statement says the officer only has a "hunch." The most logical first step is to verify if money is actually missing by recounting it (Action I). Informing the police (Action II) would be premature and unprofessional without first confirming the loss.

questions.text: Statement: Japan is not likely to grant Pakistan's request for a $500 million loan for the current year. Courses of action:I. Pakistan should approach other countries to get a loan.II. Pakistan should continue to persuade Japan to grant the loan.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: If only I is most appropriate
options.explanation: If Japan is not likely to grant the loan, Pakistan should look for alternative sources. Approaching other countries is a practical and realistic course of action.

options.text: If only II is most appropriate
options.explanation: Continuing to persuade Japan is not appropriate because the statement already indicates that Japan is unlikely to grant the loan.

options.text: If either I or II is most appropriate
options.explanation: This is incorrect because only one action (approaching other countries) is suitable under the given situation.

options.text: If both I and II are most appropriate
options.explanation: This is incorrect because persisting with Japan despite low chances is not a wise or effective action.

questions.explanation: Given the unlikelihood of the statement says Japan is unlikely to grant the loan, so persuading Japan (II) is probably futile. Approaching other countries (I) is a practical alternative.
